etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "wood") + phonetic 且(且) (qiě, "and") -> 木 with 且 stylized as 旦(旦) (dàn, "dawn") -> hawthorn
etymology.interpretation: The character 楂 (zhā) combines 木(木) (mù, "wood") as the semantic component indicating a tree or plant, with 且(且) (qiě, "and") as the phonetic component, which was stylistically adapted into 旦(旦) (dàn, "dawn"). The resulting character was used for the specific kind of tree, the hawthorn, as well as a wooden raft, both of which the compound was originally meant to describe.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢吃用新鲜的山楂制作的糖葫芦(我喜歡吃用新鮮的山楂製作的糖葫蘆)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān chī yòng xīnxiān de shānzhā zhìzuò de táng hú lu.
examples.english: I like to eat candied hawthorn made from fresh hawthorn.

examples.chinese: 山楂的味道酸甜可口(山楂的味道酸甜可口)。
examples.english: The taste of hawthorn is sweet and sour.

examples.chinese: 很多人用山楂来泡茶(很多人用山楂來泡茶)，以帮助消化(以幫助消化)。
examples.pinyin: Hěnduō rén yòng shānzhā lái pào chá，yǐ bāngzhù xiāohuà.
examples.english: Many people use hawthorn to brew tea to aid digestion.
